interview_questions: Walk me through scanning 12M sources for stolen AI-assisted art.
segment_concerns: Confused about ownership of AI-generated work
Questions whether AI-assisted work is real art
Worried about being perceived as less legitimate
Uncertain about legal standing of AI art

persona.name: Mika Tanaka
persona.background: Digital illustrator using Midjourney + manual refinement
persona.piracy_experience: Found 3 stolen pieces on Redbubble last year

answers.q1_scan_sources: I would input my portfolio URL or upload my best 20 pieces and let it crawl. Need to see the exact sites it checks - Redbubble, TeePublic, random Facebook art theft pages are my biggest headaches.
answers.q2_auto_takedown: Absolutely yes. I waste hours filing DMCA manually. Last month I spent 6 hours on takedowns instead of creating.
answers.q3_invisible_watermark: Skeptical - AI art already has artifacts. Would it degrade quality? Can it survive JPEG compression when thieves screenshot my work?
answers.q4_blockchain_timestamp: Helpful for proving I created it first, but I am confused about whether blockchain proof actually holds up legally for AI-assisted work.
answers.q5_c2pa: I have heard of C2PA but do not fully understand it. Does it prove I made the prompts and did post-processing? That is the real question.
answers.q6_missing: Protection for derivative works. Thieves take my AI-assisted art, modify it slightly, and resell. That is not covered.
answers.q7_one_feature: Bulk upload. I create 40+ variations per commission piece. I cannot upload them one by one.
answers.q8_daily_usage: Would check every morning, 5 minutes. Like checking email.
answers.q9_unhandled: Social media reposts without credit. Instagram art accounts stealing my work and getting more engagement than me.
answers.q10_how_often: Daily if automated alerts exist. Weekly manual check minimum.
answers.q11_platforms: Instagram, DeviantArt, ArtStation, Redbubble, Etsy, Pinterest
answers.q12_pieces_per_month: 15-20 finished pieces, plus 60-80 WIPs and variations
answers.q13_current_workflow: Create in Midjourney, refine in Photoshop, post to Instagram first, then DeviantArt and ArtStation. Manually reverse image search once a week on Google Images.
answers.q14_upload_barriers: Privacy concerns - do not want my prompts or process exposed. Also worried about upload limits on free tier.
answers.q15_trust: Cautious. AI analyzing AI art feels circular. Would trust more if I could see the detection methodology.
answers.q16_ai_analyzing_ai: The irony is not lost on me. But I would accept it if the analysis is transparent - show me what patterns it detects.
answers.q17_recommend: Yes, if the scanning actually covers the niche sites where art theft happens, not just Google.
answers.q18_must_do: Must prove my AI-assisted work has legitimate copyright standing before I invest time in protection.
